entries.name: Count Halfdan — deep-north-backed northern marcher lord (Skarrgaard / Cloven Strand) whose troops took you; cold war-captain, here in a wary marriage-of-convenience with Edmund against Ranulf
The Crown delegation — the Regency's people at the keep, managing the crisis and contesting the prisoners for boy-king Hadric
The Stonebay marauder-captain — leader of Ranulf's raiders you're roped in with; pragmatic, dangerous, already working his own angle for a deal or a break
Ranulf's marauders — the captured Stonebay raiding-men you've been lumped in with; the company that colors what you're taken to be
Aldwin — Count Edmund's sister-son, a young knight Shake saved from a septic spear-wound; owes his life to the leech, doesn't yet know magic was used

Wulf — Count Edmund's doomsman/reeve (the Hollowford March's lawman); six-fingered birth-mark, never gives a flat 'no'; respects Shake, now set as his keeper/jailer. GM-secret: divided loyalties — answers to the law/Crown above Edmund

Brother Oslac, the Hollow Hermit — Hollowford's beloved ascetic, secretly a deep-cover Bone-Sayer agent (hidden origins); vouched the assassin to Aldwin's bedside. Caught (Perception 19), in irons, defiant and SILENT ('weighed and paid'). Tic; won't name his masters
Count Edmund — STRICKEN by apoplexy (Scene 6), half-paralyzed & aphasic, alive but out of command; Shake's patient and lord, passing the weight to Aldwin
Wystan — Count Edmund's son & rightful heir; FAR away (with the war-muster) and somehow shadowed (hidden origins/secret); a looming succession factor not yet on the scene

Captain Garmund — Edmund's household war-captain; commands the Hollowford muster, will fight the coming war; harbors an irrational loathing of Shake (the drift-man with too much sway) — a present rival
Maerwald — Count Edmund's court-mage; a real spellcaster, watching Shake with professional suspicion — the likeliest to see through the leech to the drift-marked kept-class secret
